name: Temporal Equivalence Principle: A Unified Resolution to the JWST High-Redshift Anomalies
description: JWST has revealed high-redshift anomalies whose strongest current TEP tests are the large-sample photometric lines, the direct kinematic comparison, and the structural selectivity of the Uniformity Paradox. Star formation efficiencies exceeding $\Lambda$CDM limits and anomalous stellar-to-dynamical mass ratios both appear preferentially in deep gravitational potentials, while overmassive black holes in Little Red Dots are retained as an unresolved compact-core stress test rather than as a primary closure result. Using the Observable Response Coefficient \kappa_{\rm gal} = (9.6 \pm 4.0) \times 10^5 mag measured by Paper 11's Cepheid analysis, applied directly to the potential-linear \Gamma_t formula with no JWST-specific refit, the framework quantitatively accounts for the leading excess under the TEP response mapping. Keywords: Cosmology: early universe – Galaxies: high-redshift – Galaxies: evolution – Gravitation – Temporal Topology – Infrared: galaxies
